---
title: 'PBMC analysis'
author:
- name: Anson Wong
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
description: null
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
xaringanExtra::use_panelset()

```

# 1 Load packages

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(Seurat)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(igraph)
library(harmony)
library(here)
library(qs)
library(forcats)
library(pals)
library(ggplot2)
library(speckle)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(Cepo)
library(phateR)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(clustree)
library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)
library(SeuratDisk)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scran)
library(sceasy)
library(schard)
library(BiocParallel)
library(data.table)
library(Matrix)
library(edgeR)
library(limma)
library(RUVSeq)
library(readr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(future)
library(future.apply)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(enrichplot)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(cowplot)
})
options(future.globals.maxSize = 500000000000, future.seed=TRUE)
set.seed(1990)

```

```{r}
# prepare colours
colours <- c(brewer.paired(12), rev(brewer.pastel1(9)),  brewer.pastel2(8), brewer.spectral(11), 
             rev(brewer.accent(8)), rev(brewer.dark2(8)), 
             brewer.set1(9), brewer.pastel2(8), brewer.spectral(11), 
             brewer.brbg(11)[1:5],rev(brewer.set3(12)),brewer.brbg(11)[6:11],
             brewer.piyg(11)[1:5],brewer.piyg(11)[7:11], brewer.piyg(11)[1:5],brewer.piyg(11)[7:11])

Condition2.colours <- setNames(c("#26547c","#ffd166","#ef476f"),
                               c("C","CFN","CFB"))
```


# 2 Prepare objects
## 2.1 Load objects
```{r}
seu <- qread(here("data","SCEs","analysis","pbmc.SEU.qs"),nthreads=24)
```


# 3 Cepo
```{r}
# Cepo
cepoMarkers <- Cepo(seu[["SCT"]]@data,
                    seu$ann_lv1,
                    exprsPct = 0.2)
cepoMarkers$stats

cepoMarkers$stats <- cepoMarkers$stats[!grepl('^A[C|F|P|L][0|1]', row.names(cepoMarkers$stats)),]

sapply(1:ncol(cepoMarkers$stats), function(i){
  names(sort(cepoMarkers$stats[,i], decreasing = TRUE))[1:300]
}) %>% data.frame() -> dat2

colnames(dat2) <- colnames(cepoMarkers$stats)
dat2

dat2 %>% data.frame() %>% write.csv(file=here("data","pbmc_ann_lv1.markers.csv"),row.names = FALSE)
```


# 4 DE
## 4.1 Pseudobulk
```{r}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"

out_dir <- here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix")
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# store pseudobulk Seurat objects here
pb_list <- list()

# meta.data (avoid piping a Seurat object by accident)
md <- seu[[]]
cells <- rownames(md)

safe_name <- function(x) {
  # replace anything that's not alnum, dot, underscore, or dash with underscore
  str_replace_all(x, "[^A-Za-z0-9._-]", "_")
}

# Choose workers (rule of thumb: <= number of cell types and <= CPU cores)
workers <- min(20, parallel::detectCores())  # change 8 to what your machine allows

# Linux: multicore is fastest; Windows: use multisession
# plan(multicore, workers = workers)     # Linux/macOS
plan(multisession, workers = workers)    # portable

for (x in levels(seu$ann_lv2)) {
  
  if (x == "Ambiguous") {
    message("[SKIP] ", x, ": skipping Ambiguous level")
    next
  }

  per_sample <- md %>%
    filter(ann_lv2 == x) %>%
    dplyr::count(sampleID, name = "n")

  if (nrow(per_sample) == 0) {
    message("[SKIP] ", x, ": no cells found")
    next
  }

  if (any(per_sample$n < 3)) {
    bad <- paste(per_sample$sampleID[per_sample$n < 3], collapse = ", ")
    message("[SKIP] ", x, ": <3 cells in sample(s): ", bad)
    next
  }

  s <- subset(seu, subset = ann_lv2 == x)
  DefaultAssay(s) <- "RNA"

  pseudo_bulk <- AggregateExpression(
    s, assays = "RNA", slot = "counts",
    group.by = "ID2",
    return.seurat = TRUE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )
  
  pseudo_bulk <- NormalizeData(pseudo_bulk,verbose = FALSE)
  pb_list[[x]] <- pseudo_bulk
  message("[OK] ", x, ": stored pseudobulk (", ncol(pseudo_bulk), " groups)")

  # pb_mat <- GetAssayData(pseudo_bulk, assay = "RNA", slot = "counts")
  # 
  # fn <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(safe_name(x), "_counts.csv"))
  # write_csv(
  #   rownames_to_column(as.data.frame(as.matrix(pb_mat), check.names = FALSE), "gene"),
  #   fn
  # )
  # 
  # message("[WRITE] ", x, " -> ", basename(fn))
}

```

## 4.2 RUVseq [sex]
```{r}
## 4.2 RUVseq  (with Sex + confounding check; minimal changes)
# ----------------------------
# Config
# ----------------------------
pb_dir  <- here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix")
ruv_dir <- here("data", "DE", "RUV_sex2")

k_ruv <- 5
min_max_count <- 10

dir.create(ruv_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

workers <- min(20, parallel::detectCores())
plan(multisession, workers = workers)

# ----------------------------
# Helpers
# ----------------------------
read_counts_csv <- function(path) {
  dat <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  gene_col <- if ("gene" %in% names(dat)) "gene" else if ("Gene" %in% names(dat)) "Gene" else names(dat)[1]

  counts <- dat %>%
    dplyr::select(-all_of(gene_col)) %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)

  counts <- as.matrix(sapply(counts, as.numeric))
  rownames(counts) <- dat[[gene_col]]

  list(counts = counts, gene_col = gene_col)
}

# Uses your known ID2->Sex mapping from the table.
# If any sampleID is missing here, it errors (so you notice immediately).
make_sample_info <- function(sample_ids) {
  sex_map <- c(
    "C-1"="Female","C-2"="Female","C-3"="Female","C-4"="Male","C-5"="Male",
    "CFN-1"="Male","CFN-2"="Female","CFN-3"="Male","CFN-4"="Male","CFN-5"="Male","CFN-6"="Female",
    "CFB-1"="Male","CFB-2"="Male","CFB-3"="Female","CFB-4"="Male","CFB-5"="Female","CFB-6"="Male"
  )

  si <- tibble(sampleID = sample_ids) %>%
    mutate(
      group3 = case_when(
        str_starts(sampleID, "C-")   ~ "C",
        str_starts(sampleID, "CFN-") ~ "CFN",
        str_starts(sampleID, "CFB-") ~ "CFB",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      Sex = unname(sex_map[sampleID])
    )

  if (any(is.na(si$group3))) {
    stop("Cannot parse group for: ", paste(si$sampleID[is.na(si$group3)], collapse = ", "))
  }
  if (any(is.na(si$Sex))) {
    stop("Cannot parse Sex for: ", paste(si$sampleID[is.na(si$Sex)], collapse = ", "),
         "\nAdd these sampleIDs to sex_map in make_sample_info().")
  }

  si$Sex <- factor(si$Sex, levels = c("Female", "Male"))
  si
}

# returns list(design=..., msg="...") and decides whether Sex is estimable
make_design_with_optional_sex <- function(sample_info) {
  sample_info$group <- droplevels(factor(sample_info$group))
  sample_info$Sex   <- droplevels(factor(sample_info$Sex))

  design0 <- model.matrix(~0 + group, data = sample_info)
  colnames(design0) <- sub("^group", "", colnames(design0))

  # only try adding Sex if both sexes are present
  if (nlevels(sample_info$Sex) < 2) {
    return(list(design = design0, msg = "[DESIGN] Sex has <2 levels; using group-only design"))
  }

  design1 <- model.matrix(~0 + group + Sex, data = sample_info)
  colnames(design1) <- sub("^group", "", colnames(design1))

  # check estimability (full rank)
  if (qr(design1)$rank < ncol(design1)) {
    # Sex is confounded (or near-confounded) with group in this subset
    return(list(
      design = design0,
      msg = paste0(
        "[DESIGN] Sex confounded with group (rank-deficient); using group-only design. group x Sex = ",
        paste(capture.output(print(table(sample_info$group, sample_info$Sex))), collapse = " ")
      )
    ))
  }

  list(
    design = design1,
    msg = paste0(
      "[DESIGN] Using group+Sex design. group x Sex = ",
      paste(capture.output(print(table(sample_info$group, sample_info$Sex))), collapse = " ")
    )
  )
}

run_ruvr_edger <- function(counts, sample_info, contrast_expr, out_csv,
                           k_ruv = 5, min_max_count = 10, gene_col = "gene") {
  keep <- apply(counts, 1, max) >= min_max_count
  counts <- counts[keep, , drop = FALSE]

  dge <- DGEList(counts = counts)
  dge <- calcNormFactors(dge, method = "TMM")

  # build design (group + Sex if estimable)
  des <- make_design_with_optional_sex(sample_info)
  design <- des$design

  cont <- limma::makeContrasts(contrasts = contrast_expr, levels = design)

  # initial fit for residuals used by RUVr
  y0 <- estimateDisp(dge, design)
  fit0 <- glmQLFit(y0, design)

  ruv <- RUVSeq::RUVr(as.matrix(counts), rownames(counts), k_ruv,
                      residuals = residuals(fit0))

  design_ruv <- cbind(design, ruv$W)
  cont_ruv <- rbind(cont, matrix(0, nrow = ncol(ruv$W), ncol = ncol(cont)))

  y1 <- estimateDisp(dge, design_ruv)
  fit1 <- glmQLFit(y1, design_ruv)
  qlf <- glmQLFTest(fit1, contrast = cont_ruv)

  out <- topTags(qlf, n = Inf, sort.by = "none")$table

  res <- out %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = gene_col) %>%
    dplyr::rename(AveExpr = logCPM, P.Value = PValue, adj.P.Val = FDR) %>%
    arrange(adj.P.Val, P.Value) %>%
    filter(adj.P.Val < 0.1)

  readr::write_csv(res, out_csv)
  invisible(list(res = res, design_msg = des$msg))
}

contrasts <- list(
  CFvsC  = list(groups = c("C", "CF"),  expr = "CF - C"),
  CFNvsC = list(groups = c("C", "CFN"), expr = "CFN - C"),
  CFBvsC = list(groups = c("C", "CFB"), expr = "CFB - C"),
  CFBvsCFN = list(groups = c("CFB", "CFN"), expr = "CFB - CFN")
)

# ----------------------------
# Work for one file (one cell type)
# ----------------------------
process_one_celltype <- function(f) {
  celltype <- sub("_counts\\.csv$", "", basename(f))
  cell_out_dir <- file.path(ruv_dir, celltype)
  dir.create(cell_out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  obj <- read_counts_csv(f)
  counts <- obj$counts
  gene_col <- obj$gene_col

  si_all <- make_sample_info(colnames(counts))

  results <- list(celltype = celltype, ok = TRUE, messages = character())

  for (nm in names(contrasts)) {
    spec <- contrasts[[nm]]

    si <- si_all %>%
      mutate(group = case_when(
        nm == "CFvsC" & group3 != "C" ~ "CF",
        TRUE ~ group3
      )) %>%
      filter(group %in% spec$groups) %>%
      mutate(group = factor(group, levels = spec$groups))

    counts_sub <- counts[, si$sampleID, drop = FALSE]
    out_csv <- file.path(cell_out_dir, paste0(celltype, "_", nm, ".csv"))

    msg <- tryCatch({
      ret <- run_ruvr_edger(counts_sub, si, spec$expr, out_csv,
                            k_ruv = k_ruv, min_max_count = min_max_count, gene_col = gene_col)
      paste0("[WRITE] ", basename(out_csv), " ", ret$design_msg)
    }, error = function(e) {
      paste0("[FAIL] ", celltype, " ", nm, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
    })

    results$messages <- c(results$messages, msg)
  }

  results
}

# ----------------------------
# Parallel run
# ----------------------------
files <- sort(list.files(pb_dir, pattern = "_counts\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE))

log <- future.apply::future_lapply(files, process_one_celltype, future.seed = TRUE)

cat(paste(unlist(lapply(log, `[[`, "messages")), collapse = "\n"), "\n")
message("Done. Outputs under: ", ruv_dir)

```

## 4.3 GSEA
```{r}
# -------
# Config
# ----------------------------
ruv_dir <- here("data", "DE", "RUV_sex2")
gsea_root <- here("data", "gsea_sex2")
dir.create(gsea_root, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

workers <- min(20, parallel::detectCores())
plan(multisession, workers = workers)   # use multicore on linux if you want

# find all DE csvs under data/DE/RUV/<celltype>/*.csv
csv_files <- sort(list.files(ruv_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE))
if (length(csv_files) == 0) stop("No CSV files found under: ", ruv_dir)

# ----------------------------
# Worker: run gseGO on one file (ONE run on all genes; interpret NES sign later)
# ----------------------------
run_one_gsego <- function(de_csv, gsea_root) {
  celltype <- basename(dirname(de_csv))
  compare  <- str_remove(basename(de_csv), "^.*?_") %>% str_remove("\\.csv$")  # e.g. CFNvsC
  prefix   <- str_remove(basename(de_csv), "\\.csv$")                         # e.g. CD4T.naive_CFNvsC

  # read DE
  de <- readr::read_csv(de_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)

  # required columns check
  if (!all(c("gene", "logFC") %in% names(de))) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = FALSE, msg = "[SKIP] missing gene/logFC"))
  }

  # build ranking (tutorial style)
  gene_list <- de$logFC
  names(gene_list) <- de$gene
  gene_list <- gene_list[!is.na(gene_list) & !is.na(names(gene_list)) & names(gene_list) != ""]
  gene_list <- sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)

  if (length(gene_list) < 10) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = FALSE, msg = "[SKIP] too few ranked genes"))
  }

  # run GSEA (GO BP)
  set.seed(1)
  gsea_res <- tryCatch(
    gseGO(
      geneList     = gene_list,
      OrgDb        = org.Hs.eg.db,
      keyType      = "SYMBOL",
      ont          = "BP",
      minGSSize    = 5,
      pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
      verbose      = FALSE
    ),
    error = function(e) e
  )

  if (inherits(gsea_res, "error") || is.null(gsea_res)) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = FALSE, msg = paste0("[FAIL] ", conditionMessage(gsea_res))))
  }

  gsea_tbl <- as.data.frame(gsea_res) %>% arrange(p.adjust)
  if (nrow(gsea_tbl) == 0) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = TRUE, msg = "[SKIP] no enriched pathways (empty result)"))
  }

  # Keep only significant (you used pvalueCutoff=0.05 already, but be defensive)
  gsea_tbl_sig <- gsea_tbl %>% filter(p.adjust < 0.1)
  if (nrow(gsea_tbl_sig) == 0) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = TRUE, msg = "[SKIP] no enriched pathways (padj<0.1 none)"))
  }

  # Split into "up" vs "down" by NES sign (recommended way)
  up_tbl <- gsea_tbl_sig %>% filter(NES > 0) %>% arrange(p.adjust)
  dn_tbl <- gsea_tbl_sig %>% filter(NES < 0) %>% arrange(p.adjust)

  # If neither direction has rows (unlikely), skip writing
  if (nrow(up_tbl) == 0 && nrow(dn_tbl) == 0) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = TRUE, msg = "[SKIP] no NES>0 or NES<0 rows after filtering"))
  }

  # Create output subdir per celltype ONLY if there is something to write
  out_dir <- file.path(gsea_root, celltype)
  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  # Write (only if non-empty)
  wrote <- character()

  if (nrow(up_tbl) > 0) {
    out_up <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(prefix, "_gsea_UP.csv"))
    readr::write_csv(up_tbl, out_up)
    wrote <- c(wrote, basename(out_up))
  }

  if (nrow(dn_tbl) > 0) {
    out_dn <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(prefix, "_gsea_DOWN.csv"))
    readr::write_csv(dn_tbl, out_dn)
    wrote <- c(wrote, basename(out_dn))
  }

  list(
    file = de_csv,
    ok = TRUE,
    msg = paste0("[WRITE] ", celltype, " ", compare, " -> ", paste(wrote, collapse = ", "))
  )
}

# ----------------------------
# Parallel run (one DE csv per worker)
# ----------------------------
log <- future.apply::future_lapply(
  csv_files,
  run_one_gsego,
  gsea_root = gsea_root,
  future.seed = TRUE
)

cat(paste(vapply(log, `[[`, "", "msg"), collapse = "\n"), "\n")
message("Done. Outputs under: ", gsea_root)
```

## 4.4 GO overrepresentation
```{r}

# ---- config ----
ruv_dir <- here("data", "DE", "RUV_sex")
out_dir <- here("data", "go_sex")
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

workers <- min(20, parallel::detectCores())   # adjust to your quota
plan(multisession, workers = workers)         # use multicore on linux if you prefer

# ---- find all RUV DE csvs ----
ruv_csvs <- sort(list.files(ruv_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE))
ruv_csvs <- ruv_csvs[str_detect(basename(ruv_csvs), "_(CFvsC|CFNvsC|CFBvsC)\\.csv$")]

if (length(ruv_csvs) == 0) stop("No RUV DE CSV files found under: ", ruv_dir)

# ---- worker function: process one DE csv ----
process_one_ruv_de <- function(de_csv, out_dir) {
  celltype <- basename(dirname(de_csv))
  compare  <- str_match(basename(de_csv), "_(CFvsC|CFNvsC|CFBvsC)\\.csv$")[,2]

  de <- readr::read_csv(de_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)

  # guards / cleanup
  if (!all(c("gene", "logFC") %in% names(de))) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = FALSE, msg = "Missing gene/logFC columns"))
  }

  de <- de %>% filter(!is.na(gene), gene != "", !is.na(logFC))
  if (nrow(de) == 0) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = FALSE, msg = "Empty after filtering NA/blank genes"))
  }

  up   <- de %>% filter(logFC > 0) %>% distinct(gene)
  down <- de %>% filter(logFC < 0) %>% distinct(gene)

  # map SYMBOL -> ENTREZ for just this file
  genes_all <- unique(de$gene)
  sym2ent <- AnnotationDbi::mapIds(
    x = org.Hs.eg.db,
    keys = genes_all,
    keytype = "SYMBOL",
    column = "ENTREZID",
    multiVals = "first"
  )

  to_entrez <- function(sym_vec) {
    entrez <- unname(sym2ent[sym_vec])
    entrez <- entrez[!is.na(entrez)]
    unique(entrez)
  }

  up_entrez       <- to_entrez(up$gene)
  down_entrez     <- to_entrez(down$gene)
  universe_entrez <- to_entrez(unique(de$gene))

  if (length(universe_entrez) < 10) {
    return(list(file = de_csv, ok = FALSE, msg = "Too few mapped universe genes"))
  }

  # GO BP ORA
  go.up.bp <- enrichGO(
    gene          = up_entrez,
    universe      = universe_entrez,
    OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
    keyType       = "ENTREZID",
    ont           = "BP",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
    readable      = TRUE
  )

  go.down.bp <- enrichGO(
    gene          = down_entrez,
    universe      = universe_entrez,
    OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
    keyType       = "ENTREZID",
    ont           = "BP",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
    readable      = TRUE
  )

  # simplify (only when results exist)
  if (!is.null(go.up.bp) && nrow(go.up.bp@result) > 0)   go.up.bp <- clusterProfiler::simplify(go.up.bp)
  if (!is.null(go.down.bp) && nrow(go.down.bp@result) > 0) go.down.bp <- clusterProfiler::simplify(go.down.bp)

  mk_df <- function(go_obj, direction) {
    if (!is.null(go_obj) && nrow(go_obj@result) > 0) {
      go_obj@result %>%
        dplyr::rename(Pathway = Description) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(
          Celltype = celltype,
          Comparison = compare,
          Direction = direction,
          Genes = gsub("/", "; ", geneID)
        ) %>%
        dplyr::select(Pathway, Celltype, Comparison, Direction, ID, Count, pvalue, p.adjust, Genes) %>%
        dplyr::slice_head(n = 50)
    } else {
      tibble::tibble()
    }
  }

  up_df   <- mk_df(go.up.bp, "Up")
  down_df <- mk_df(go.down.bp, "Down")

  fn_up   <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(celltype, "_", compare, "_GO_BP-up_top50.csv"))
  fn_down <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(celltype, "_", compare, "_GO_BP-down_top50.csv"))

  readr::write_csv(up_df, fn_up)
  readr::write_csv(down_df, fn_down)

  list(file = de_csv, ok = TRUE,
       msg = paste0("Wrote: ", basename(fn_up), " and ", basename(fn_down)))
}

# ---- parallel run ----
log <- future.apply::future_lapply(
  ruv_csvs,
  process_one_ruv_de,
  out_dir = out_dir,
  future.seed = TRUE
)

cat(paste(vapply(log, `[[`, "", "msg"), collapse = "\n"), "\n")
message("Done. GO ORA results under: ", out_dir)
```


## 4.5 Pseudobulk gene expression
### 4.5.1 Plot Function
```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Reusable: Plot pseudobulk expression as log2CPM (TMM-normalized)
# + significance brackets from EXISTING RUVr/edgeR results
# (NO Wilcoxon)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# ----------------------------
# Read counts helper
# ----------------------------
read_counts_csv <- function(path) {
  dat <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  gene_col <- if ("gene" %in% names(dat)) "gene" else if ("Gene" %in% names(dat)) "Gene" else names(dat)[1]

  counts <- dat |>
    dplyr::select(-dplyr::all_of(gene_col)) |>
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)

  counts <- as.matrix(sapply(counts, as.numeric))
  rownames(counts) <- dat[[gene_col]]

  list(counts = counts, gene_col = gene_col)
}

# ----------------------------
# Make log2CPM (TMM) once
# ----------------------------
make_log2cpm <- function(counts, method = "TMM", prior.count = 1) {
  counts <- as.matrix(counts)
  dge <- edgeR::DGEList(counts = counts)
  dge <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(dge, method = method)
  edgeR::cpm(dge, log = TRUE, prior.count = prior.count)
}

# ----------------------------
# Read DE (RUVr/edgeR) helpers
# ----------------------------
read_de_csv <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) stop("DE file not found: ", path)
  de <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)

  gene_col <- if ("gene" %in% names(de)) "gene" else if ("Gene" %in% names(de)) "Gene" else names(de)[1]
  if (!"adj.P.Val" %in% names(de) && "FDR" %in% names(de)) {
    de <- de |> dplyr::rename(adj.P.Val = FDR)
  }
  if (!"adj.P.Val" %in% names(de)) stop("Cannot find adj.P.Val (or FDR) in: ", path)

  de |>
    dplyr::rename(gene = dplyr::all_of(gene_col)) |>
    dplyr::select(gene, dplyr::everything())
}

# mimic rstatix significance symbols
p_to_signif <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("ns")
  if (p <= 0.001) return("***")
  if (p <= 0.01)  return("**")
  if (p <= 0.05)  return("*")
  if (p <= 0.1)   return(".")
  "ns"
}

# build pb_df for ONE gene (used for y-position and plotting)
build_pb_df_for_gene <- function(pb_mat, gene, seu, condition_levels = c("C", "CFB", "CFN")) {
  if (!gene %in% rownames(pb_mat)) stop("gene '", gene, "' not found in pb_mat rownames")

  meta_df <- seu@meta.data
  if (!"ID2" %in% colnames(meta_df)) stop("seu@meta.data must contain 'ID2'")
  if (!"Condition2" %in% colnames(meta_df)) stop("seu@meta.data must contain 'Condition2'")

  gene_col <- paste0(gene, "_expr")

  pb_df <- data.frame(ID2 = colnames(pb_mat), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  pb_df[[gene_col]] <- as.numeric(pb_mat[gene, , drop = TRUE])

  map_df <- meta_df[, c("ID2", "Condition2"), drop = FALSE]
  map_df <- map_df[!is.na(map_df$ID2) & !is.na(map_df$Condition2), , drop = FALSE]

  id2_to_cond <- tapply(as.character(map_df$Condition2), map_df$ID2, function(x) {
    ux <- sort(table(x), decreasing = TRUE)
    names(ux)[1]
  })

  pb_df$Condition2 <- unname(id2_to_cond[match(pb_df$ID2, names(id2_to_cond))])
  pb_df$Condition2 <- factor(pb_df$Condition2, levels = condition_levels)

  pb_df
}

# Build an rstatix-like table for ONE gene (for stat_pvalue_manual)
make_sig_table_from_ruv <- function(pb_df, gene, de_tbls,
                                    comparisons = c("CFBvsC", "CFNvsC", "CFBvsCFN"),
                                    step = 0.5, hide_ns = TRUE) {

  gene_col <- paste0(gene, "_expr")
  ymax <- max(pb_df[[gene_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(ymax)) ymax <- 0

  comp_map <- list(
    CFBvsC   = c("CFB", "C"),
    CFNvsC   = c("CFN", "C"),
    CFBvsCFN = c("CFB", "CFN")
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_along(comparisons), function(i) {
    nm <- comparisons[i]
    gpair <- comp_map[[nm]]
    if (is.null(gpair)) stop("Unknown comparison: ", nm)

    de <- de_tbls[[nm]]
    if (is.null(de)) stop("Missing DE table for comparison '", nm, "'")

    p_adj <- de$adj.P.Val[match(gene, de$gene)]
    if (length(p_adj) == 0 || is.na(p_adj)) p_adj <- 1

    data.frame(
      group1 = gpair[1],
      group2 = gpair[2],
      p.adj = as.numeric(p_adj),
      p.adj.signif = p_to_signif(as.numeric(p_adj)),
      y.position = ymax + step * i,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }) |>
    dplyr::bind_rows()

  if (hide_ns) out <- out |> dplyr::filter(.data$p.adj.signif != "ns")
  out
}

# Plot function (no Wilcoxon; uses supplied p-value table)
plot_pb_gene_ruv <- function(pb_mat, gene, seu, p_tbl = NULL,
                             pal = NULL, zero_filter = TRUE, dodge_width = 0.8,
                             ylab = "log2CPM",
                             condition_levels = c("C", "CFB", "CFN")) {

  pb_df <- build_pb_df_for_gene(pb_mat = pb_mat, gene = gene, seu = seu, condition_levels = condition_levels)

  gene_col <- paste0(gene, "_expr")

  # filtering
  if (zero_filter) {
    pb_df <- pb_df[!is.na(pb_df[[gene_col]]) & pb_df[[gene_col]] != 0 & !is.na(pb_df$Condition2), ]
  } else {
    pb_df <- pb_df[!is.na(pb_df[[gene_col]]) & !is.na(pb_df$Condition2), ]
  }
  if (nrow(pb_df) == 0) stop("No samples available after filtering/matching (check pb_mat colnames are ID2)")

  # palette
  if (is.null(pal)) {
    if (exists("Condition2.colours")) pal <- get("Condition2.colours")
    else if (exists("condition2_cols")) pal <- get("condition2_cols")
  }
  if (is.null(pal)) {
    levs <- levels(pb_df$Condition2)
    pal <- setNames(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "Dark2")[seq_along(levs)], levs)
  }

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(pb_df, ggplot2::aes(x = Condition2, y = .data[[gene_col]], color = Condition2)) +
    ggplot2::geom_boxplot(fill = NA, outlier.shape = NA, position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = dodge_width), size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
    ggplot2::labs(y = ylab, title = gene) +
    ggplot2::theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.x      = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1, size = 10, colour = "black"),
      axis.text.y      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
      axis.title.y     = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title.x     = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.line        = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.border     = ggplot2::element_rect(size = 0.5, fill = "transparent"),
      legend.position  = "none",
      plot.title       = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "italic")
    )

  if (!is.null(p_tbl) && nrow(p_tbl) > 0) {
    p <- p + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(
      p_tbl,
      label = "p.adj.signif",
      tip.length = 0.01,
      bracket.size = 0.4,
      hide.ns = TRUE
    )
  }

  p
}

# ----------------------------
# MAIN reusable wrapper you asked for
# ----------------------------
plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk <- function(
  celltype,
  de_base_dir,                 # e.g. here("data","DE","RUV_sex2") OR here("data","DE","bal_ann_lv2")
  genes,
  seu,
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix"),
  comparisons = c("CFBvsC", "CFNvsC", "CFBvsCFN"),
  condition_levels = c("C", "CFB", "CFN"),
  step = 0.5,
  hide_ns = TRUE,
  zero_filter = FALSE,
  ylab = "log2CPM"
) {
  if (missing(celltype) || !nzchar(celltype)) stop("celltype must be a non-empty string")
  if (missing(genes) || length(genes) == 0) stop("genes must be a non-empty character vector")
  if (missing(seu)) stop("seu is required")

  # auto paths from celltype + base dirs
  pb_counts_path <- file.path(pb_base_dir, paste0(celltype, "_counts.csv"))
  if (!file.exists(pb_counts_path)) stop("Pseudobulk counts file not found: ", pb_counts_path)

  de_dir <- file.path(de_base_dir, celltype)

  de_files <- list(
    CFBvsC   = file.path(de_dir, paste0(celltype, "_CFBvsC.csv")),
    CFNvsC   = file.path(de_dir, paste0(celltype, "_CFNvsC.csv")),
    CFBvsCFN = file.path(de_dir, paste0(celltype, "_CFBvsCFN.csv"))
  )

  # read + normalize
  obj <- read_counts_csv(pb_counts_path)
  pb_mat_log2cpm <- make_log2cpm(obj$counts, method = "TMM", prior.count = 1)
  
  # ensure output dir exists
  # write log2CPM matrix (TMM-normalized) for this celltype
  log2cpm_out_path <- file.path(pb_base_dir, paste0(celltype, "_log2cpm_TMM.csv"))
  
  log2cpm_df <- data.frame(
    gene = rownames(pb_mat_log2cpm),
    pb_mat_log2cpm,
    check.names = FALSE
  )
  
  readr::write_csv(log2cpm_df, log2cpm_out_path)
  message("Wrote log2CPM matrix to: ", log2cpm_out_path)

  # read DE once (only comparisons requested)
  de_files <- de_files[comparisons]
  de_tbls <- lapply(de_files, read_de_csv)

  # plots
  plot_list <- lapply(genes, function(g) {
    pb_df_tmp <- build_pb_df_for_gene(pb_mat = pb_mat_log2cpm, gene = g, seu = seu, condition_levels = condition_levels)
    pb_df_tmp <- pb_df_tmp[!is.na(pb_df_tmp[[paste0(g, "_expr")]]) & !is.na(pb_df_tmp$Condition2), , drop = FALSE]

    p_tbl <- make_sig_table_from_ruv(
      pb_df = pb_df_tmp,
      gene = g,
      de_tbls = de_tbls,
      comparisons = comparisons,
      step = step,
      hide_ns = hide_ns
    )

    plot_pb_gene_ruv(
      pb_mat = pb_mat_log2cpm,
      gene = g,
      seu = seu,
      p_tbl = p_tbl,
      zero_filter = zero_filter,
      ylab = ylab,
      condition_levels = condition_levels
    )
  })

  names(plot_list) <- genes
  plot_list
}

```

### 4.5.2 plot
```{r fig.width=5,fig.height=2.5}
# plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
#   celltype = "CD8T",
#   pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix_bal_ann_lv1"),
#   de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv1"),  # PBMC
#   genes = genes,
#   seu = seu.bal
# )

# ----------------------------
# PBMC CD14.mono
# ----------------------------
genes <- c("GZMK","KLRG1","ITGAE")

plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
  celltype = "CD8T",
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix_bal_ann_lv1"),
  de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv1"),  # PBMC
  genes = genes,
  seu = seu.bal
)

# (B) Arrange as a nice grid with patchwork
wrap_plots(plot.list, ncol = 3)

# BAL example:
# plot.list.cd14.bal <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
#   celltype = "CD14.mono",
#   de_base_dir = here::here("data", "DE", "bal_ann_lv2"),
#   genes = genes,
#   seu = s
# )
# 1) remove per-panel axis titles
plots <- lapply(plot.list, \(p) {
  p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y = element_blank())
})

# 2) make the grid
pg <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots, ncol = 3, align = "hv")

left_pad <- 0.02  # increase for more space (try 0.08, 0.10)

p_shared <- ggdraw() +
  # draw the grid shifted right to create a left margin
  draw_plot(pg, x = left_pad, y = 0, width = 1 - left_pad, height = 1) +
  # now the y label can sit in that left margin without clipping
  draw_label("log2CPM", x = 0.02, y = 0.5, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = 12)

p_shared

```

```{r fig.width=10,fig.height=2.5}
# plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
#   celltype = "CD8T",
#   pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix_bal_ann_lv1"),
#   de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv1"),  # PBMC
#   genes = genes,
#   seu = seu.bal
# )

# ----------------------------
# PBMC CD14.mono
# ----------------------------
genes <- c("PLA2G7","SPP1","CHI3L1")

plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
  celltype = "MDM-PLA2G7hi",
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix_bal_ann_lv2"),
  de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv2"),  # PBMC
  genes = genes,
  seu = seu.bal
)

# (B) Arrange as a nice grid with patchwork
wrap_plots(plot.list, ncol = 5)

# BAL example:
# plot.list.cd14.bal <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
#   celltype = "CD14.mono",
#   de_base_dir = here::here("data", "DE", "bal_ann_lv2"),
#   genes = genes,
#   seu = s
# )
# 1) remove per-panel axis titles
plots <- lapply(plot.list, \(p) {
  p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y = element_blank())
})

# 2) make the grid
pg <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots, ncol = 5, align = "hv")

left_pad <- 0.02  # increase for more space (try 0.08, 0.10)

p_shared <- ggdraw() +
  # draw the grid shifted right to create a left margin
  draw_plot(pg, x = left_pad, y = 0, width = 1 - left_pad, height = 1) +
  # now the y label can sit in that left margin without clipping
  draw_label("log2CPM", x = 0.02, y = 0.5, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = 12)

p_shared

```

```{r fig.width=12,fig.height=9}
genes <- c("GZMK","KLRG1","CD27","EOMES","ITGAE")

plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
  celltype = "cd8t",
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data"),
  de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv1"),  # PBMC
  genes = genes,
  seu = seu.bal
)

# ----------------------------
# PBMC CD14.mono
# ----------------------------
genes <- c("GZMK","KLRG1","CD27","EOMES","ITGAE")

plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
  celltype = "CD8T",
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix"),
  de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv1"),  # PBMC
  genes = genes,
  seu = seu.bal
)

# (B) Arrange as a nice grid with patchwork
wrap_plots(plot.list, ncol = 3)

# BAL example:
# plot.list.cd14.bal <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
#   celltype = "CD14.mono",
#   de_base_dir = here::here("data", "DE", "bal_ann_lv2"),
#   genes = genes,
#   seu = s
# )
# 1) remove per-panel axis titles
plots <- lapply(plot.list, \(p) {
  p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y = element_blank())
})

# 2) make the grid
pg <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots, ncol = 3, align = "hv")

left_pad <- 0.02  # increase for more space (try 0.08, 0.10)

p_shared <- ggdraw() +
  # draw the grid shifted right to create a left margin
  draw_plot(pg, x = left_pad, y = 0, width = 1 - left_pad, height = 1) +
  # now the y label can sit in that left margin without clipping
  draw_label("log2CPM", x = 0.02, y = 0.5, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = 12)

p_shared
```

### 4.5.3 Dot plot
```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=2}
# PBMC CD14.mono
md <- seu.pbmc@meta.data
cells.keep <- rownames(md)[md$ann_lv2 %in% c("CD14.mono")]
s <- subset(seu.pbmc, cells = cells.keep)
s$ann_lv1 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv1)
s$ann_lv2 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv2)
selected <- c("CXCL8","EREG","SERPINB2","F13A1","CDC42BPA","NRG1")

# BAL cDC
md <- seu.bal@meta.data
cells.keep <- rownames(md)[md$ann_lv2 %in% c("cDC")]
s <- subset(seu.bal, cells = cells.keep)
s$ann_lv1 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv1)
s$ann_lv2 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv2)
selected <- c(
  "CCL4", "CCL3", "CCL2", "CXCL10", "CCL3L1",
  "IFI6", "IFIT3",
  "CTSL", "CTSB", "LGMN",
  "HLA-DQA2",
  "XCR1", "CLEC9A", "IRF8"
)


p5 <- DotPlot(s,
        features = selected,
        group.by = "Condition2",
        col.max = 3,
        col.min = 0,
        dot.scale = 6
        ) + #coord_flip() +
  geom_point(aes(size=pct.exp), shape = 21, colour="black", stroke=0.5) +
  FontSize(y.text = 10, x.text = 8) + 
  labs(y = element_blank(), x = element_blank()) + 
  theme(text=element_text(family="Ariel"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size=10,
                                   face="italic",
                                   angle = 45,
                                   hjust = 1,
                                   vjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=10),
        legend.direction="horizontal",
        legend.position="bottom",
        legend.justification = "right",
        legend.box="vertical",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8,family = "Arial"),
        ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = brewer.bugn(9)[1], high=brewer.bugn(9)[8]) +
  scale_size_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), 
                        breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
                        labels=c(0,"",50,"",100)) +
  guides(size=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21, 
                                             colour="black", 
                                             fill="#adb5bd"),
                                             title="% of exp. cells",
                                             label.position="bottom",
                                             title.position="top",
                                             title.hjust=0.5
                                             ))

p5$guides$colour$title <- "Mean Expression"
p5$guides$colour$title.position <- "top"
p5$guides$colour$title.hjust <- 0.5

suppressWarnings(p5)

```

## 4.6 Pseudobulk % cells expressing genes
### 4.6.1 Plot Function
```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Reusable: Plot pseudobulk % cells expressing a gene
# (%cells with RAW COUNT > 0)
# + significance brackets from EXISTING RUVr/edgeR results
# (NO Wilcoxon)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# ----------------------------
# Read counts helper (unchanged)
# ----------------------------
read_counts_csv <- function(path) {
  dat <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  gene_col <- if ("gene" %in% names(dat)) "gene" else if ("Gene" %in% names(dat)) "Gene" else names(dat)[1]

  counts <- dat |>
    dplyr::select(-dplyr::all_of(gene_col)) |>
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)

  counts <- as.matrix(sapply(counts, as.numeric))
  rownames(counts) <- dat[[gene_col]]

  list(counts = counts, gene_col = gene_col)
}

# ----------------------------
# Read DE (RUVr/edgeR) helpers (unchanged)
# ----------------------------
read_de_csv <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) stop("DE file not found: ", path)
  de <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)

  gene_col <- if ("gene" %in% names(de)) "gene" else if ("Gene" %in% names(de)) "Gene" else names(de)[1]
  if (!"adj.P.Val" %in% names(de) && "FDR" %in% names(de)) {
    de <- de |> dplyr::rename(adj.P.Val = FDR)
  }
  if (!"adj.P.Val" %in% names(de)) stop("Cannot find adj.P.Val (or FDR) in: ", path)

  de |>
    dplyr::rename(gene = dplyr::all_of(gene_col)) |>
    dplyr::select(gene, dplyr::everything())
}

# mimic rstatix significance symbols (unchanged)
p_to_signif <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("ns")
  if (p <= 0.001) return("***")
  if (p <= 0.01)  return("**")
  if (p <= 0.05)  return("*")
  if (p <= 0.1)   return(".")
  "ns"
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build pb_df for ONE gene as % expressing based on RAW COUNTS > 0
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
build_pb_pct_df_for_gene_raw <- function(pb_counts, gene, seu,
                                         condition_levels = c("C", "CFB", "CFN"),
                                         count_cutoff = 0) {

  if (!gene %in% rownames(pb_counts)) stop("gene '", gene, "' not found in pb_counts rownames")

  meta_df <- seu@meta.data
  if (!"ID2" %in% colnames(meta_df)) stop("seu@meta.data must contain 'ID2'")
  if (!"Condition2" %in% colnames(meta_df)) stop("seu@meta.data must contain 'Condition2'")

  gene_col <- paste0(gene, "_pct")

  pb_df <- data.frame(ID2 = colnames(pb_counts), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  # NOTE: This is "expressing in pseudobulk sample" as binary (count > cutoff) * 100.
  pb_df[[gene_col]] <- as.numeric(pb_counts[gene, , drop = TRUE] > count_cutoff) * 100

  # map ID2 -> dominant Condition2 (unchanged logic)
  map_df <- meta_df[, c("ID2", "Condition2"), drop = FALSE]
  map_df <- map_df[!is.na(map_df$ID2) & !is.na(map_df$Condition2), , drop = FALSE]

  id2_to_cond <- tapply(as.character(map_df$Condition2), map_df$ID2, function(x) {
    ux <- sort(table(x), decreasing = TRUE)
    names(ux)[1]
  })

  pb_df$Condition2 <- unname(id2_to_cond[match(pb_df$ID2, names(id2_to_cond))])
  pb_df$Condition2 <- factor(pb_df$Condition2, levels = condition_levels)

  pb_df
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Significance table: y-position based on % scale
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
make_sig_table_from_ruv_pct <- function(pb_df, gene, de_tbls,
                                        comparisons = c("CFBvsC", "CFNvsC", "CFBvsCFN"),
                                        step = 5, hide_ns = TRUE) {

  gene_col <- paste0(gene, "_pct")
  ymax <- max(pb_df[[gene_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(ymax)) ymax <- 0

  comp_map <- list(
    CFBvsC   = c("CFB", "C"),
    CFNvsC   = c("CFN", "C"),
    CFBvsCFN = c("CFB", "CFN")
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_along(comparisons), function(i) {
    nm <- comparisons[i]
    gpair <- comp_map[[nm]]
    if (is.null(gpair)) stop("Unknown comparison: ", nm)

    de <- de_tbls[[nm]]
    if (is.null(de)) stop("Missing DE table for comparison '", nm, "'")

    p_adj <- de$adj.P.Val[match(gene, de$gene)]
    if (length(p_adj) == 0 || is.na(p_adj)) p_adj <- 1

    data.frame(
      group1 = gpair[1],
      group2 = gpair[2],
      p.adj = as.numeric(p_adj),
      p.adj.signif = p_to_signif(as.numeric(p_adj)),
      y.position = ymax + step * i,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }) |>
    dplyr::bind_rows()

  if (hide_ns) out <- out |> dplyr::filter(.data$p.adj.signif != "ns")
  out
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot function: y is % expressing (RAW COUNT > count_cutoff)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
plot_pb_gene_ruv_pct_raw <- function(pb_counts, gene, seu, p_tbl = NULL,
                                     pal = NULL, zero_filter = FALSE, dodge_width = 0.8,
                                     ylab = "% samples with count > 0",
                                     condition_levels = c("C", "CFB", "CFN"),
                                     count_cutoff = 0) {

  pb_df <- build_pb_pct_df_for_gene_raw(
    pb_counts = pb_counts, gene = gene, seu = seu,
    condition_levels = condition_levels,
    count_cutoff = count_cutoff
  )

  gene_col <- paste0(gene, "_pct")

  # filtering (keep same structure; note: 0 is meaningful now)
  if (zero_filter) {
    pb_df <- pb_df[!is.na(pb_df[[gene_col]]) & pb_df[[gene_col]] != 0 & !is.na(pb_df$Condition2), ]
  } else {
    pb_df <- pb_df[!is.na(pb_df[[gene_col]]) & !is.na(pb_df$Condition2), ]
  }
  if (nrow(pb_df) == 0) stop("No samples available after filtering/matching (check pb_counts colnames are ID2)")

  # palette (unchanged)
  if (is.null(pal)) {
    if (exists("Condition2.colours")) pal <- get("Condition2.colours")
    else if (exists("condition2_cols")) pal <- get("condition2_cols")
  }
  if (is.null(pal)) {
    levs <- levels(pb_df$Condition2)
    pal <- setNames(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "Dark2")[seq_along(levs)], levs)
  }

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(pb_df, ggplot2::aes(x = Condition2, y = .data[[gene_col]], color = Condition2)) +
    ggplot2::geom_boxplot(fill = NA, outlier.shape = NA, position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = dodge_width), size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 100)) +
    ggplot2::labs(y = ylab, title = gene) +
    ggplot2::theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.x      = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1, size = 10, colour = "black"),
      axis.text.y      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
      axis.title.y     = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title.x     = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.line        = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.border     = ggplot2::element_rect(size = 0.5, fill = "transparent"),
      legend.position  = "none",
      plot.title       = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold.italic")
    )

  if (!is.null(p_tbl) && nrow(p_tbl) > 0) {
    p <- p + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(
      p_tbl,
      label = "p.adj.signif",
      tip.length = 0.01,
      bracket.size = 0.4,
      hide.ns = TRUE
    )
  }

  p
}

# ----------------------------
# MAIN reusable wrapper (raw count > 0)
# ----------------------------
plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk_pct_raw <- function(
  celltype,
  de_base_dir,
  genes,
  seu,
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix"),
  comparisons = c("CFBvsC", "CFNvsC", "CFBvsCFN"),
  condition_levels = c("C", "CFB", "CFN"),
  step = 5,
  hide_ns = TRUE,
  zero_filter = FALSE,
  ylab = "% samples with count > 0",
  count_cutoff = 0
) {
  if (missing(celltype) || !nzchar(celltype)) stop("celltype must be a non-empty string")
  if (missing(genes) || length(genes) == 0) stop("genes must be a non-empty character vector")
  if (missing(seu)) stop("seu is required")

  pb_counts_path <- file.path(pb_base_dir, paste0(celltype, "_counts.csv"))
  if (!file.exists(pb_counts_path)) stop("Pseudobulk counts file not found: ", pb_counts_path)

  de_dir <- file.path(de_base_dir, celltype)

  de_files <- list(
    CFBvsC   = file.path(de_dir, paste0(celltype, "_CFBvsC.csv")),
    CFNvsC   = file.path(de_dir, paste0(celltype, "_CFNvsC.csv")),
    CFBvsCFN = file.path(de_dir, paste0(celltype, "_CFBvsCFN.csv"))
  )

  # read RAW counts (no normalization)
  obj <- read_counts_csv(pb_counts_path)
  pb_counts <- obj$counts

  # read DE once (only comparisons requested)
  de_files <- de_files[comparisons]
  de_tbls <- lapply(de_files, read_de_csv)

  plot_list <- lapply(genes, function(g) {
    pb_df_tmp <- build_pb_pct_df_for_gene_raw(
      pb_counts = pb_counts, gene = g, seu = seu,
      condition_levels = condition_levels,
      count_cutoff = count_cutoff
    )
    pb_df_tmp <- pb_df_tmp[!is.na(pb_df_tmp[[paste0(g, "_pct")]]) & !is.na(pb_df_tmp$Condition2), , drop = FALSE]

    p_tbl <- make_sig_table_from_ruv_pct(
      pb_df = pb_df_tmp,
      gene = g,
      de_tbls = de_tbls,
      comparisons = comparisons,
      step = step,
      hide_ns = hide_ns
    )

    plot_pb_gene_ruv_pct_raw(
      pb_counts = pb_counts,
      gene = g,
      seu = seu,
      p_tbl = p_tbl,
      zero_filter = zero_filter,
      ylab = ylab,
      condition_levels = condition_levels,
      count_cutoff = count_cutoff
    )
  })

  names(plot_list) <- genes
  plot_list
}

```

### 4.6.2 plot
```{r fig.width=12,fig.height=9}
# ----------------------------
# PBMC CD14.mono
# ----------------------------
genes <- c("KLRB1","GZMK","XCL2")

plot.list <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk_pct(
  celltype = "CD8T",
  pb_base_dir = here::here("data", "pseudobulk_matrix_bal_ann_lv1"),
  de_base_dir = here("data", "DE","RUV_bal_ann_lv1"),  # PBMC
  genes = genes,
  seu = seu.bal
)

# (B) Arrange as a nice grid with patchwork
wrap_plots(plot.list, ncol = 3)

# BAL example:
# plot.list.cd14.bal <- plot_pb_genes_ruv_from_disk(
#   celltype = "CD14.mono",
#   de_base_dir = here::here("data", "DE", "bal_ann_lv2"),
#   genes = genes,
#   seu = s
# )
# 1) remove per-panel axis titles
plots <- lapply(plot.list, \(p) {
  p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y = element_blank())
})

# 2) make the grid
pg <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots, ncol = 3, align = "hv")

left_pad <- 0.02  # increase for more space (try 0.08, 0.10)

p_shared <- ggdraw() +
  # draw the grid shifted right to create a left margin
  draw_plot(pg, x = left_pad, y = 0, width = 1 - left_pad, height = 1) +
  # now the y label can sit in that left margin without clipping
  draw_label("log2CPM", x = 0.02, y = 0.5, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = 12)

p_shared
```

### 4.6.3 Dot plot
```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=2}
# PBMC CD14.mono
md <- seu.pbmc@meta.data
cells.keep <- rownames(md)[md$ann_lv2 %in% c("CD14.mono")]
s <- subset(seu.pbmc, cells = cells.keep)
s$ann_lv1 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv1)
s$ann_lv2 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv2)
selected <- c("CXCL8","EREG","SERPINB2","F13A1","CDC42BPA","NRG1")

# BAL cDC
md <- seu.bal@meta.data
cells.keep <- rownames(md)[md$ann_lv2 %in% c("cDC")]
s <- subset(seu.bal, cells = cells.keep)
s$ann_lv1 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv1)
s$ann_lv2 <- fct_drop(s$ann_lv2)
selected <- c(
  "CCL4", "CCL3", "CCL2", "CXCL10", "CCL3L1",
  "IFI6", "IFIT3",
  "CTSL", "CTSB", "LGMN",
  "HLA-DQA2",
  "XCR1", "CLEC9A", "IRF8"
)


p5 <- DotPlot(s,
        features = selected,
        group.by = "Condition2",
        col.max = 3,
        col.min = 0,
        dot.scale = 6
        ) + #coord_flip() +
  geom_point(aes(size=pct.exp), shape = 21, colour="black", stroke=0.5) +
  FontSize(y.text = 10, x.text = 8) + 
  labs(y = element_blank(), x = element_blank()) + 
  theme(text=element_text(family="Ariel"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size=10,
                                   face="italic",
                                   angle = 45,
                                   hjust = 1,
                                   vjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=10),
        legend.direction="horizontal",
        legend.position="bottom",
        legend.justification = "right",
        legend.box="vertical",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8,family = "Arial"),
        ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = brewer.bugn(9)[1], high=brewer.bugn(9)[8]) +
  scale_size_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), 
                        breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
                        labels=c(0,"",50,"",100)) +
  guides(size=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21, 
                                             colour="black", 
                                             fill="#adb5bd"),
                                             title="% of exp. cells",
                                             label.position="bottom",
                                             title.position="top",
                                             title.hjust=0.5
                                             ))

p5$guides$colour$title <- "Mean Expression"
p5$guides$colour$title.position <- "top"
p5$guides$colour$title.hjust <- 0.5

suppressWarnings(p5)

```



# 5 Save figures
```{r}
celltype <- "PBMC_CD14.mono"
out <- here("data","plots",celltype)
if(!dir.exists(out)) {
  dir.create(out,recursive = TRUE)
}

showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 1600)

# CXCL8 box plot (PBMC)
for (p in names(plot.list.cd14)) {
  ggsave(plot=plot.list.cd14[[p]] + theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial")),
         device="jpeg",
         dpi=1600,
         filename = paste0(out,"/",paste0("pbmc_CD14.mono_",p,"_boxplot.jpeg")),
         height=4,
         width=3.5)
}
showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 100)



showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 1600)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  device="jpeg",
  filename = paste0(out,"/",paste0("DEG_boxplot_PBMC_CD14.mono.jpeg")),
  plot = p_shared,
  width = 8, 
  height = 6, 
  dpi = 1600
)

showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 100)

celltype <- "BAL_CD8T"
out <- here("data","plots",celltype)
if(!dir.exists(out)) {
  dir.create(out,recursive = TRUE)
}

showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 1600)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  device="jpeg",
  filename = paste0(out,"/",paste0("DEG_boxplot_BAL_CD8T.jpeg")),
  plot = p_shared,
  width = 6, 
  height = 2.5, 
  dpi = 1600
)

showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 100)



celltype <- "Tcell"
out <- here("data","plots",celltype)
if(!dir.exists(out)) {
  dir.create(out,recursive = TRUE)
}

showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 1600)

# test set dotplot 
layout = "AA\nBB"
p1 + p2 +
  plot_layout(design = layout) +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial"))

ggsave(plot=last_plot(),
       device="jpeg",
       dpi=1600,
       filename = paste0(out,"/","ttest_dotplot.jpeg"),
       height=6,
       width=6)

# T test umap grouped by Naive Bayes classifier
p8 +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial"))

ggsave(plot=last_plot(),
       device="jpeg",
       dpi=1600,
       filename = paste0(out,"/","ttest_umap.jpeg"),
       height=3,
       width=8)

# T cell umap grouped by Naive Bayes classifier
p3 +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial"))

ggsave(plot=last_plot(),
       device="jpeg",
       dpi=1600,
       filename = paste0(out,"/","T_umap.jpeg"),
       height=3,
       width=8)

# merged set dotplot 
layout = "AA\nBB"
p4 + p5 +
  plot_layout(design = layout) +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial"))

ggsave(plot=last_plot(),
       device="jpeg",
       dpi=1600,
       filename = paste0(out,"/","T_dotplot.jpeg"),
       height=6,
       width=6)

# feature plot (BAL)
p6 +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial"))

ggsave(plot=last_plot(),
       device="jpeg",
       dpi=1600,
       filename = paste0(out,"/","balT_featureplot.jpeg"),
       height=8,
       width=12)

# feature plot (PBMC)
p7 +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", family="Arial"))

ggsave(plot=last_plot(),
       device="jpeg",
       dpi=1600,
       filename = paste0(out,"/","pbmcT_featureplot.jpeg"),
       height=8,
       width=12)

showtext::showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 100)
```


# 6 Save object
```{r}
date <- Sys.Date()
out <- here("data","SCEs","analysis",paste0("analysis_",date,".SEU.qs")) # 2025-12-22
qsave(seu,file=out,nthreads=24)

```
